proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +36.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +36.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +36.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with 100% Choppy exposure across 25 open positions, so activation is deferred and existing concentration is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-28) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type×sector + by_signal_type = Choppy (100%) + UP_TRI×Choppy×FMCG (24%) + UP_TRI (96%) (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type×sector + by_signal_type = Choppy (100%) + UP_TRI×Choppy×FMCG (24%) + UP_TRI (96%); recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and sector=FMCG until FMCG cell share drops below 0.20 (via natural Day-6 exits) AND consider hedging the long-Choppy book with a short index/futures position equivalent to ~25-30% of gross exposure to neutralize Choppy→Bear tail risk.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate all 25 long positions with no statistical edge backing (Candidate-tier cohort only), with concentrated drawdown in FMCG (24%) and Pharma (16%) sectors compounding the unwind.

evidence: UP_TRI×Choppy×FMCG cell at 24% breaches 0.20 cell threshold (6/25 positions)
UP_TRI signal share at 24/25 = 96% breaches 0.85 single-signal threshold
Matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort only at Candidate tier (n=144) — unvalidated edge
Three additional cells near threshold: Pharma 16%, Metal 12%, Other 12%
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI and sector=FMCG until FMCG cell share drops below 0.20 (via natural Day-6 exits) AND consider hedging the long-Choppy book with a short index/futures position equivalent to ~25-30% of gross exposure to neutralize Choppy→Bear tail risk.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip would simultaneously invalidate all 25 long positions with no statistical edge backing (Candidate-tier cohort only), with concentrated drawdown in FMCG (24%) and Pharma (16%) sectors compounding the unwind.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (observed 1.0, above the 0.80 threshold) AND the UP_TRI×Choppy×FMCG cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 24%. The matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=144) — unvalidated edge. UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 24/25 = 96% (above the 0.85 threshold), and the book is fully directional long, so a Choppy→Bear flip would unwind the entire 25-position book simultaneously.
